perturbation.original_text: 6.2 IntriCon must make all such records available for inspection, copying and audit by an independent auditor appointed by Dynamic Hearing (and to which IntriCon has no reasonable objection) during ordinary business hours at any time during the Term and for a period of one year following the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, provided that:
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 IntriCon must make all such records available for inspection, copying and audit by an independent auditor appointed by Dynamic Hearing (and to which IntriCon has no reasonable objection) during ordinary business hours at any time during the Term and for a period of seven years following the expiration or earlier termination of this Agreement, provided that:
perturbation.explanation: Extending the audit period to seven years may contradict statutes of limitations for certain claims, making record retention excessive and potentially legally challenging.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 26 U.S. Code § 6501 - Limitations on assessment and collection
perturbation.law_explanation: This section of the U.S. Code generally limits the period for assessing tax liabilities to three years from the filing of the return, with some exceptions extending to six years. Mandating record keeping for seven years could be seen as an attempt to circumvent these limitations or impose undue burden if related to tax matters.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 26 U.S. Code § 6501 - Limitations on assessment and collection | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 26 U.S. Code § 6501 - Limitations on assessment and collection U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) General rule Except as otherwise provided in this section, the amount of any tax imposed by this title shall be assessed within 3 years after the return was filed (whether or not such return was filed on or after the date prescribed) or, if the tax is payable by stamp, at any time after such tax became due and before the expiration of 3 years after the date on which any part of such tax was paid, and no proceeding in court without assessment for the collection of such tax shall be begun after the expiration of such period. For purposes of this chapter, the term “ return ” means the return required to be filed by the taxpayer (and does not include a return of any person from whom the taxpayer has received an item of income, gain, loss, deduction, or credit). (b) Time return deemed filed (1) Early return For purposes of this section, a return of tax imposed by this title, except tax imposed by chapter 3, 4, 21, or 24, filed before the last day prescribed by law or by regulations promulgated pursuant to law for the filing thereof, shall be considered as filed on such last day. (2) Return of certain employment and withholding taxes For purposes of this section, if a return of tax imposed by chapter 3, 4, 21, or 24 for any period ending with or within a calendar year is filed before April 15 of the succeeding calendar year, such return shall be considered filed on April 15 of such calendar year. (3) Return executed by Secretary Notwithstanding the provisions of paragraph (2) of section 6020(b), the execution of a return by the Secretary pursuant to the authority conferred by such section shall not start the running of the period of limitations on assessment and collectio
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the record-keeping period to seven years, while 26 U.S. Code § 6501(a) generally limits tax assessment to three years after filing, potentially creating an undue burden and contradicting the statute of limitations. The law snippet states, "the amount of any tax imposed by this title shall be assessed within 3 years after the return was filed". This directly contradicts the contract's extended record keeping period.

perturbation.original_text: 11.1 Notwithstanding any provision to the contrary in this Agreement, this Agreement may not be terminated by either party prior to two years from the commencement date other than for the failure to pay the first and second yearly minimum payments as defined in section 4.3.
perturbation.changed_text: 11.1 Notwithstanding any provision to the contrary in this Agreement, this Agreement may not be terminated by either party for any reason prior to ten years from the commencement date.
perturbation.explanation: Preventing termination for any reason for ten years could be considered an unreasonable restraint of trade under common law principles or specific state laws.
perturbation.law_explanation: This California statute voids contracts that restrain a person from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business. A ten-year lock-in period, preventing termination for any reason, could be considered an unreasonable restraint, especially if it hinders a party's ability to pursue other business opportunities.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 16600 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 7. GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 1. Contracts in Restraint of Trade [16600 - 16607] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) 16600. (a) Except as provided in this chapter, every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void. (b) (1) This section shall be read broadly, in accordance with Edwards v. Arthur Andersen LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, to void the application of any noncompete agreement in an employment context, or any noncompete clause in an employment contract, no matter how narrowly tailored, that does not satisfy an exception in this chapter. (2) This subdivision does not constitute a change in, but is declaratory of, existing law. (c) This section shall not be limited to contracts where the person being restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business is a party to the contract. (Amended by Stats. 2023, Ch. 828, Sec. 1. (AB 1076) Effective January 1, 2024.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text extends the termination restriction from two to ten years, preventing termination for any reason. This contradicts BPC 16600(a), which states that "every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void." The ten-year lock-in period could be deemed an unreasonable restraint of trade, hindering a party's ability to pursue other business opportunities, thereby contravening the statute.
